 0.000000+0 0.000000+0          0          0        139          21525 1451    4
 15-P - 31 LANL       EVAL-DEC97 M.CHADWICK,P.YOUNG               1525 1451    5
 CH97,CH99            DIST-AUG24 REV1-NOV19            20240830   1525 1451    6
---- ENDF/B-VIII.1    MATERIAL 1525         REVISION 1            1525 1451    7
----- INCIDENT-PROTON DATA                                        1525 1451    8
------ ENDF-6                                                     1525 1451    9
                                                                  1525 1451   10
Oct 22,2004, Holly Trellue remade mf6mt5 using corrected gnash    1525 1451   11
code, to fix an earlier bug. Impact is reduced sec. particle prod.1525 1451   12
for new ENDF/B-VII release                                        1525 1451   13
 **************************************************************** 1525 1451   14
                                                                  1525 1451   15
 ENDF/B-VI MOD 1 Evaluation, December 1997, M.B. Chadwick and     1525 1451   16
                 P.G. Young (LANL)                                1525 1451   17
                                                                  1525 1451   18
 Produced with FKK/GNASH/GSCAN code in cooperation with ECN Petten1525 1451   19
                                                                  1525 1451   20
  This evaluation provides a complete representation of the       1525 1451   21
nuclear data needed for transport, damage, heating, radioactivity,1525 1451   22
and shielding applications over the incident proton energy range  1525 1451   23
from 1 to 150 MeV.  The evaluation utilizes MF=6, MT=5 to         1525 1451   24
represent all reaction data.  Production cross sections and       1525 1451   25
emission spectra are given for neutrons, protons, deuterons,      1525 1451   26
tritons, alpha particles, gamma rays, and all residual nuclides   1525 1451   27
produced (A>5) in the reaction chains. To summarize, the ENDF     1525 1451   28
sections with non-zero data above are:                            1525 1451   29
                                                                  1525 1451   30
  MF=3 MT=  2  Integral of nuclear plus interference components   1525 1451   31
               of the elastic scattering cross section            1525 1451   32
                                                                  1525 1451   33
       MT=  5  Sum of binary (p,n') and (p,x) reactions           1525 1451   34
                                                                  1525 1451   35
  MF=6 MT=  2  Elastic (p,p) angular distributions given as       1525 1451   36
               ratios of the differential nuclear-plus-           1525 1451   37
               interference to the integrated value.              1525 1451   38
                                                                  1525 1451   39
       MT=  5  Production cross sections and energy-angle         1525 1451   40
               distributions for emission neutrons, protons,      1525 1451   41
               deuterons, and alphas; and angle-integrated        1525 1451   42
               spectra for gamma rays and residual nuclei that    1525 1451   43
               are stable against particle emission               1525 1451   44
                                                                  1525 1451   45
  The evaluation is based on nuclear model calculations that have 1525 1451   46
been benchmarked to experimental data, especially for n + P31 and 1525 1451   47
p + P31 reactions [Ch98]. We use the GNASH code system [Yo92],    1525 1451   48
which utilizes Hauser-Feshbach statistical, preequilibrium and    1525 1451   49
direct-reaction theories. Spherical optical model calculations are1525 1451   50
used to obtain particle transmission coefficients for the Hauser- 1525 1451   51
Feshbach calculations, as well as for the elastic proton angular  1525 1451   52
distributions.                                                    1525 1451   53
  Cross sections and spectra for producing individual residual    1525 1451   54
nuclei are included for reactions. The energy-angle-correlations  1525 1451   55
for all outgoing particles are based on Kalbach systematics       1525 1451   56
[Ka88].                                                           1525 1451   57
  A model was developed to calculate the energy distributions of  1525 1451   58
all recoil nuclei in the GNASH calculations [Ch96a]. The recoil   1525 1451   59
energy distributions are represented in the laboratory system in  1525 1451   60
MT=5, MF=6, and are given as isotropic in the lab system. All     1525 1451   61
other data in MT=5,MF=6 are given in the center-of-mass system.   1525 1451   62
This method of representation utilizes the LCT=3 option approved  1525 1451   63
at the November, 1996, CSEWG meeting.                             1525 1451   64
  Preequilibrium corrections were performed in the course of the  1525 1451   65
GNASH calculations using the exciton model of Kalbach [Ka77,      1525 1451   66
Ka85], validated by comparison with calculations using Feshbach,  1525 1451   67
Kerman, Koonin (FKK) theory [Ch93].  Discrete level data from     1525 1451   68
nuclear data sheets were matched to continuum level densities     1525 1451   69
using the formulation of Ignatyuk et al. [Ig75] and pairing and   1525 1451   70
shell parameters from the Cook [Co67] analysis. Neutron and       1525 1451   71
charged- particle transmission coefficients were obtained from the1525 1451   72
optical potentials, as discussed below.  Gamma-ray transmission   1525 1451   73
coefficients were calculated using the Kopecky-Uhl model [Ko90].  1525 1451   74
                                                                  1525 1451   75
       SPECIFIC INFORMATION CONCERNING THE P-31 EVALUATION        1525 1451   76
                                                                  1525 1451   77
  For incident neutrons the Wilmore-Hodgson [Wi64] optical        1525 1451   78
potential was used below 20 MeV and the Madland medium [Ma88]     1525 1451   79
energy potential was used at higher energies. Above 130 MeV the   1525 1451   80
reaction cross section, and corresponding transmission            1525 1451   81
coefficients, were renormalized to agree with the evaluated       1525 1451   82
reaction cross section of [Ch96b]. For incident protons the       1525 1451   83
Becchetti-Greenlees [Be69] potential was used below 20 MeV, and   1525 1451   84
the Madland potential at higher energies. Above 6 MeV the reaction1525 1451   85
cross section (and trans. coeff.) was renormalized to values      1525 1451   86
predicted by the systematics of Wellisch [We96]. The potential of 1525 1451   87
Lohr [Lo74] was used for deuterons, and Mcfadden-Satchler [Mc66]  1525 1451   88
for alpha particles. We followed ref. [Ch96b] in ignoring He-3    1525 1451   89
emission.                                                         1525 1451   90
  Nuclear level densities were obtained using the Ignatyuk model  1525 1451   91
[Ig75], and low-lying levels were obtained after first determining1525 1451   92
the upper limit for which the experimental level information is   1525 1451   93
complete.                                                         1525 1451   94
                                                                  1525 1451   95
****************************************************************  1525 1451   96
                                                                  1525 1451   97
REFERENCES                                                        1525 1451   98
                                                                  1525 1451   99

  1190 (1969)                                                     1525 1451  101
[Ch93]  M.B. Chadwick and P.G. Young, Phys.Rev. C 47, 2255 (1993) 1525 1451  102
[Ch96a] M.B. Chadwick, P.G. Young, R.E. MacFarlane, and A.J.      1525 1451  103
  J. Koning, "High-Energy Nuclear Data Libraries for Accelerator- 1525 1451  104
  Driven Technologies:  Calculational Method for Heavy Recoils,"  1525 1451  105
  Proc. of 2nd Int. Conf. on Accelerator Driven Transmutation     1525 1451  106
  Technology and Applications, Kalmar, Sweden, 3-7 June 1996      1525 1451  107
[Ch96b] M.B. Chadwick and P.G. Young, "Proton nuclear interactions1525 1451  108
  with 12C, 14N, 16O, 31P, 40Ca for radiotherapy applications:    1525 1451  109
  evaluated data libraries up to 250 MeV", Los Alamos report      1525 1451  110
  LA-UR-96-0498                                                   1525 1451  111
[Ch98]  M. B. Chadwick and P. G. Young, "Model Calculations of    1525 1451  112
  n,p + 31P" in APT PROGRESS REPORT: 1 December 1997 - 1 January  1525 1451  113
  1998, internal Los Alamos National Laboratory memo T-2-97/MS-55,1525 1451  114
  January 1998 from R.E. MacFarlane to L. Waters.                 1525 1451  115

  National Laboratory report LA-UR-99-1222 (1999)                 1525 1451  117
[Co67]  J.L. Cook, H. Ferguson, and A.R. deL Musgrove, Aust.J.    1525 1451  118
  Phys. 20, 477 (1967)                                            1525 1451  119
[Ig75]  A. V. Ignatyuk, G. N. Smirenkin, and A. S. Tishin,        1525 1451  120
  Sov.J.Nucl.Phys. 21, 255 (1975); translated from Yad.Fiz. 21,   1525 1451  121
  485 (1975)                                                      1525 1451  122

[Ka85]  C. Kalbach, "PRECO-D2: Program for Calculating            1525 1451  124
  Preequilibrium and Direct Reaction Double Differential Cross    1525 1451  125
  Sections," Los Alamos National Laboratory report LA-10248-MS    1525 1451  126
  (1985)                                                          1525 1451  127
[Ka88]  C. Kalbach, Phys.Rev.C 37, 2350 (1988); see also          1525 1451  128
  C. Kalbach and F. M. Mann, Phys.Rev. C 23, 112 (1981)           1525 1451  129
[Ko90]  J. Kopecky and M. Uhl, Phys.Rev. C 41, 1941 (1990)        1525 1451  130
[Lo74]  J.M.Lohr and W. Haeberli, Nucl.Phys. A232, 381 (1974)     1525 1451  131
[Ma88]  D.G. Madland, "Recent Results in the Development of a     1525 1451  132
  Global Medium-Energy Nucleon-Nucleus Optical-Model Potential,"  1525 1451  133
  Proc. OECD/NEANDC Specialist's Mtg. on Preequilibrium Nuclear   1525 1451  134
  Reactions, Semmering, Austria, 10-12 Feb. 1988, NEANDC-245 'U'  1525 1451  135
  (1988)                                                          1525 1451  136
